tasks.title: Triage Issue #4: Setup Monorepo Workspace
tasks.description: Establish the sprint expectations for the monorepo workspace setup, referencing the Sprint 1 goals in the PRD.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Triaged GitHub Issue #4 by posting a comment clarifying Sprint 1 goals and expectations referencing [SHOUT-PRD.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md) and [CODING-STANDARDS.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/CODING-STANDARDS.md), and messaged the team on Slack to coordinate tasks.

thread.speaker: liam
thread.text: Hey @aiden, I just finished 'Triage Issue #4: Setup Monorepo Workspace'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Triaged GitHub Issue #4 by posting a comment clarifying Sprint 1 goals and expectations referencing [SHOUT-PRD.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md) and [CODING-STANDARDS.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/CODING-STANDARDS.md), and messaged the team on Slack to coordinate tasks.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Looks grand. Once Kong sets up the workspace, I'll review the configuration to make sure it complies with [CODING-STANDARDS.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/CODING-STANDARDS.md). We need to ensure the monorepo doesn't end up with bloated packages or formatting inconsistencies right from the start of Sprint 1 in [SHOUT-PRD.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md).

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Spot on, @aiden. I'll make sure Kong pings us on Slack once the monorepo layout is pushed so you can run your eye over the config. Once you give it the all-clear, we'll be in a good spot to crack on with the Next.js and Express base setups.

tasks.title: Verify architecture standards for Issue #4
tasks.description: Provide guidance on the directory layout, tsconfig structures, and standard files required for the monorepo workspace setup to ensure backend-frontend separation.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Provided guidance on monorepo directory layout, TypeScript configs (root base, backend, frontend), and backend database safety rules for Issue #4 on GitHub.

tasks.title: Create GitHub Issues for Sprint-1 core features
tasks.description: Create the issues for the Node Express backend base setup, Next.js frontend base setup, and basic Socket.io integration on GitHub to define the sprint tasks for Kong.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Created three GitHub issues to define Sprint 1 core tasks (Backend Express setup, Next.js frontend setup, and basic Socket.io integration) and posted a Slack message to align the team on the sprint scope.

tasks.title: Research local competitor features and monetization models
tasks.description: Analyze local competitors like Beem and Splitwise to map out Shout's competitive advantages and potential monetization paths for the product roadmap.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Researched local competitor platforms Beem and Splitwise, outlining features, monetization models, and limitations. Documented competitive advantages, monetization strategies, and product roadmap recommendations for Shout in shout_wiki/SHOUT-COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS.md, updated shout_wiki/Home.md with the corresponding link, and sent a team update to the Slack channel.

thread.speaker: chloe
thread.text: Hey @liam, I just finished 'Research local competitor features and monetization models'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Researched local competitor platforms Beem and Splitwise, outlining features, monetization models, and limitations. Documented competitive advantages, monetization strategies, and product roadmap recommendations for Shout in shout_wiki/SHOUT-COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS.md, updated shout_wiki/Home.md with the corresponding link, and sent a team update to the Slack channel.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Cracking job on this, @chloe—the breakdown of Splitwise's lack of Aussie payments and Beem's dry utility feel is spot on. Looking at those Phase 1 MVP recommendations, mate, is this fitting into the current sprint? I want to make sure we get @aiden and @kong to eyeball the dev effort on that Plaid sync before we lock anything in.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Forget the sprints for a second, Liam. Plaid sync isn't just a quick checkbox, especially if we want to avoid double-splits when transactions settle. I'll get Kong to run a tech spike on Plaid's sandbox auth and webhook sync flow first so we know what we're actually dealing with. Once he has a basic proof of concept up, we can map out the sync logic and lock in the estimate.

tasks.title: Draft WebSocket state reconciliation and resilience guidelines
tasks.description: Define the architecture and connection recovery strategies for Socket.io client-backend interaction when internet connection drops in pubs.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Created the WebSocket State Reconciliation and Resilience Guidelines wiki document, updated the project wiki Home directory with links, synchronized local copies in the workspaces, marked TASK-005 as completed, and updated the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: aiden
thread.text: Hey @sarah, I just finished 'Draft WebSocket state reconciliation and resilience guidelines'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Created the WebSocket State Reconciliation and Resilience Guidelines wiki document, updated the project wiki Home directory with links, synchronized local copies in the workspaces, marked TASK-005 as completed, and updated the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Awesome work on getting those resilience guidelines drafted, @aiden. I'll check them out today to see how they affect our implementation roadmap. Since we're trying to keep the backlog clean, mate, is this fitting into the current sprint, or should we slot the dev tickets into the next one?

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Let's slot the resilience tickets into the next sprint so Kong can focus on getting the core chat working first. No point building recovery logic until the basic socket connection is solid. I'll review his initial integration PRs when they come in.

tasks.title: Update PRD with Monetization Strategy and Competitor Insights
tasks.description: Update shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md to incorporate the monetization models (convenience fee, Shout Pro, express payout) and refine the product roadmap based on the competitor analysis.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Updated the PRD document at shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md to incorporate the monetization models (convenience fee, Shout Pro, express payout) and competitor-informed roadmap, and sent a team update to the Slack channel.

thread.speaker: liam
thread.text: Hey @aiden, I just finished 'Update PRD with Monetization Strategy and Competitor Insights'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Updated the PRD document at shout_wiki/SHOUT-PRD.md to incorporate the monetization models (convenience fee, Shout Pro, express payout) and competitor-informed roadmap, and sent a team update to the Slack channel.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Looks clean, Liam. Glad to see the convenience fee and Shout Pro tier documented properly. We'll need to ensure the technical spec has concrete error-handling patterns for these payment states so Kong doesn't run into issues when implementing the checkout. Let's make sure the database schema updates for the Pro subscription tier are mapped out before we kick off Sprint 3.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: No worries, Aiden. I'll get a ticket sorted to map out those database schema updates for the Pro tier so we're ready before Sprint 3. I'll check in with Kong on how he's tracking—is this fitting into the current sprint? Let's lock this down during tomorrow's standup.

tasks.title: Draft Plaid Integration and Webhook Sync Spec
tasks.description: Define the Plaid Sandbox authentication flow, API integration endpoints, and webhook event-driven synchronization logic to prevent duplicate splits and double charging.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Created the Plaid Integration and Webhook Sync Spec in `shout_wiki/Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md`, updated `shout_wiki/Home.md` to link the spec and update the task status to done, and posted a Slack update.

thread.speaker: aiden
thread.text: Hey @sarah, I just finished 'Draft Plaid Integration and Webhook Sync Spec'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Created the Plaid Integration and Webhook Sync Spec in `shout_wiki/Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md`, updated `shout_wiki/Home.md` to link the spec and update the task status to done, and posted a Slack update.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Legend, @aiden, appreciate you getting this sorted. I'll jump into the [Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/virtual-work/shout_wiki/Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md) to check the endpoints. Let's make sure we align on whether this is fitting into the current sprint before we get @kong started on the implementation.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Too right, Liam. I'd rather we didn't cram the [Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/virtual-work/shout_wiki/Plaid-Integration-and-Webhook-Sync-Spec.md) into this sprint if Kong is already flat out. Let's get the scope sorted first, and once you've locked it in, I'll walk him through the webhook logic.

tasks.title: Draft Shout Investor Pitch Deck Outline
tasks.description: Create a pitch deck outline for pre-seed funding, covering the market problem, competitor gaps (Beem/Splitwise), solution value prop, monetization paths, and financial milestones.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Drafted the pre-seed investor pitch deck outline for Shout, focusing on market problems, competitive gaps between Beem and Splitwise, our solution's core value proposition, multi-layered monetization paths, viral growth loops, and financial milestones. Updated the Home wiki page to link the new document and marked TASK-008 as complete. Shared an update with the team in Slack.

thread.speaker: chloe
thread.text: Hey @liam, I just finished 'Draft Shout Investor Pitch Deck Outline'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Drafted the pre-seed investor pitch deck outline for Shout, focusing on market problems, competitive gaps between Beem and Splitwise, our solution's core value proposition, multi-layered monetization paths, viral growth loops, and financial milestones. Updated the Home wiki page to link the new document and marked TASK-008 as complete. Shared an update with the team in Slack.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Nice work on the outline, @chloe—the positioning against Beem and Splitwise is spot on. I'm a bit wary of the milestone timelines on Slide 9, is this fitting into the current sprint? @sarah, would love your thoughts on the monetization streams on Slide 7 when you have a sec.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Spot on, Liam. Those milestones on Slide 9 are way too aggressive, and we shouldn't be promising venue integrations or OCR subscriptions before we've even got the core payment flow running reliably. Let's hold off on finalizing the roadmap until Sarah reviews the monetization on Slide 7, then we can plan a realistic path forward.

tasks.title: Draft Sprint 1 OKR and Milestone Alignment Review
tasks.description: Write a high-level strategy review evaluating Sprint-1 progress against the investor demo milestone, highlighting business readiness, risk factors, and strategic priorities.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Drafted the Sprint 1 OKR and Milestone Alignment Review document in the wiki, updated the Home wiki page to link it and update the task status, and shared a sprint focus update with the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: sarah
thread.text: Hey @liam, I just finished 'Draft Sprint 1 OKR and Milestone Alignment Review'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Drafted the Sprint 1 OKR and Milestone Alignment Review document in the wiki, updated the Home wiki page to link it and update the task status, and shared a sprint focus update with the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: The [Sprint-1_OKR_and_Milestone_Alignment_Review.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/virtual-work/shout_wiki/Sprint-1_OKR_and_Milestone_Alignment_Review.md) looks spot on, @sarah. Deferring the WebSocket resilience stuff to Sprint 2 to keep @kong focused on the baseline chat is definitely the right call for the demo. But if Chloe starts pushing for UI polish early, mate, is this fitting into the current sprint? I'll monitor the board daily to make sure we don't drift from the core payment loops.

thread.speaker: Sarah
thread.text: If Chloe starts pushing for early UI polish, shut it down. Unless it directly impacts signup or payment conversion, how does this move the needle on our MVP demo? Let's keep Kong focused on the baseline chat and payment loop so we can land a flawless run for the investors.

tasks.title: Review pitch deck outline monetization and milestones
tasks.description: Review Slide 7 (monetization streams) and Slide 9 (milestones) of the pre-seed investor pitch deck outline, aligning them with business goals and dev timelines.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Reviewed the investor pitch deck monetization streams and milestones, created the review wiki document, updated Home.md, marked TASK-010 as done in agent_tasks.json, and messaged the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: sarah
thread.text: I've reviewed Slide 7 and Slide 9 of the pitch deck outline and documented the changes in [Pitch-Deck-Monetization-and-Milestones-Review.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/virtual-work/shout_wiki/Pitch-Deck-Monetization-and-Milestones-Review.md). @liam, we need to clarify that the 1.5% take rate is a net markup above Stripe's costs, not a flat convenience fee, otherwise we're bleeding margin. @chloe, I've pushed the Pro tier features to Q4 to keep @kong focused on the core MVP chat. Let's make sure our milestones reflect this.

tasks.title: Create ticket for Pro tier database schema updates
tasks.description: Draft the database schema requirements and user stories for the Shout Pro subscription tier, ensuring alignment before Sprint 3 planning.
tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Created GitHub issue outlining Shout Pro subscription database requirements, including user stories, proposed TypeScript interfaces, indexing strategies, and security validation boundaries. Sent a Slack message to coordinate alignment and clarify that these changes are strictly for backlog planning and not current sprint scope.

thread.speaker: liam
thread.text: Hey @aiden, I just finished 'Create ticket for Pro tier database schema updates'. Could you take a look and let me know your thoughts? Summary: Created GitHub issue outlining Shout Pro subscription database requirements, including user stories, proposed TypeScript interfaces, indexing strategies, and security validation boundaries. Sent a Slack message to coordinate alignment and clarify that these changes are strictly for backlog planning and not current sprint scope.

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Good stuff, Liam. Keeping this strictly in the backlog is the right call—Kong needs to focus on the core chat sync for now. I'll look over the ticket's interfaces and indexing strategies tonight. We'll need to make sure the subscription states are cached properly so we don't hammer the database on every API request.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Spot on, @aiden, keeping Kong focused on the chat sync is key so we don't have to ask, is this fitting into the current sprint? Let me know your thoughts on the caching and indexing when you've had a look tonight, and we can lock this in for the Sprint 3 backlog.

tasks.creator: planner
tasks.deliverable_ref: Created the SHOUT-TECH-SPEC wiki page documenting core database schemas, Stripe fee calculations, card declines, timeouts, webhook reconciliation flows, and shared TypeScript types. Notified the team on Slack.

Summary: Created the SHOUT-TECH-SPEC wiki page documenting core database schemas, Stripe fee calculations, card declines, timeouts, webhook reconciliation flows, and shared TypeScript types. Notified the team on Slack.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Nice one, @aiden, appreciate you getting those payment error-handling flows documented in the tech spec. I'll review it today to make sure the implementation details are clear for @kong. Since we've got a tight timeline, is this fitting into the current sprint?

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Yeah, it's done and dusted, so it's ready for the sprint. Make sure Kong goes over the webhook reconciliation details closely; we need to be absolutely certain what happens when the db connection drops. I'll review his initial PR as soon as he submits it.

thread.speaker: Liam
thread.text: Nice one, Chloe. I'll jump onto the wiki and review those guidelines now. Once we're happy with the flow, I'll sync with @aiden to map out the implementation tasks for Kong, but mate, is this fitting into the current sprint?

thread.speaker: Aiden
thread.text: Had a look at the [Plaid-Onboarding-and-Verification-Guidelines.md](file:///Users/kong/Desktop/Develop/VirtualWork/virtual-work/shout_wiki/Plaid-Onboarding-and-Verification-Guidelines.md) and they look decent, despite Chloe's dig at my clean layouts. Don't worry about booking a meeting to map tasks; just throw them in Slack and I'll review them when my migration builds are running. It should fit the sprint if we keep the scope tight, but we need to make sure we've got solid try-catch blocks for the token exchange endpoints before Kong starts coding.
